assertions.title: Partial and case-insensitive matching

steps.observed: Returned 14 results, matching lower-case 'test'. PASS for case-insensitivity.

Returned 0 results. 'test_5' does not match 'test_5s' via FTS5. 'test_30' also returns 0 results (title is 'test_30s'). However 'group' matches 'group-c-corrupt-test' (10 results) and 'corrupt' matches (3 results).
Returned 0 results. FTS5 does not support prefix matching by default.

assertions.issues: Partial/prefix matching does NOT work for all cases. FTS5 with unicode61 tokenizer matches whole tokens only. Titles with underscores (test_5s) are tokenized as single tokens, so 'test_5' doesn't match 'test_5s'. However, hyphenated titles (group-c-corrupt-test) are split into individual tokens by the tokenizer. Case-insensitive matching DOES work correctly (TEST matches test). The root cause: FTS5 unicode61 tokenizer treats underscores as part of the token but hyphens as delimiters.

frictions.description: FTS5 with unicode61 tokenizer does not handle prefix/partial matching for tokens containing underscores. 'test_5' does not match 'test_5s' because the underscore is treated as part of the token, not a delimiter. Hyphens ARE treated as delimiters, so 'group' matches 'group-c-corrupt-test'.
frictions.resolution: This is a known FTS5 tokenizer behavior. To support prefix matching, FTS5 would need the '*' prefix search operator or a custom tokenizer.

summary: Tested 16 assertions: 13 passed, 1 failed (VAL-SEARCH-006: partial/prefix matching doesn't work for underscore-containing tokens), 2 skipped (VAL-SEARCH-010: M5 scope, VAL-SEARCH-013: no active jobs). Case-insensitive matching works correctly. All special characters handled safely. Performance excellent at 9ms.
